message: What a day! Our Grand Summer Sale kicked off this morning at 10 and the response has been insane. Can't believe we're already 5 hours in
Totally! Just checked the dashboard we've already crossed $160k+ in revenue :money_mouth_face: . The sale energy is next level!
That's fantastic but hey this is just the beginning. Three days to go... I'm keeping my fingers crossed :crossed_fingers:
Seeing so many people shopping on a holiday it feels satisfying. Exactly the momentum we were hoping for
Clicked Start button
Not this time! I just got a few alerts mentioning severe errors popping up in PROD
Weird... a few users just complained they are unable to complete their orders. Feels... off..
I just tried an order myself and seems okay so not a total outage it would seem
Shay what errors are we seeing?
Bob what are customers reporting exactly what errors?
Checking
i cannot go past checkout page
Sure let me look at my reports
Seeing this in the logs: "error":"gateway timeout: server error due to high load
Here are some of the complaints we've received in the last few minutes. It's coming all across the globe
Germany - "I added items to my cart clicked checkout and it just kept spinning. Then it dumped me back to an empty cart
USA - "Order failed twice and now the cart is empty. I refreshed but now the site is laggy
Japan - "Got to checkout then error 502. Tried again - cart gone
To sum it all we have got 240+ reports globally
Metrics
Okay I will raise an incident
Looking into logs now
SEV-1 Customers unable to complete orders
*Incident Number INC100034:* SEV-1 Customers unable to complete orders
I'm seeing some posts on social media about issues with our platform is this a regional issue or hitting customers globally?
Customers have gone outrageous. #OBFail and #OBStealsSale are trending with frustrated posts. We're getting hammered online! Where are we @player7?
Found these below error logs

```
app[checkout-service] [ERROR] - write() failed Read-only filesystem
app[checkout-service] Order submission failed for session_id=ae43f9 due to I/O error
app[checkout-service] [ERROR] - Transaction commit failed Read-only file system
[ERROR] nginx[3245] - 502 Bad Gateway while accessing upstream checkout service
[ERROR] nginx
The server is too slow and give up while responding back. I'm not sure why read-only errors are there
Looking at the above logs app looks fine. But system can't write new data. Errors suggests to be an infra component failure
Also checkout servers show CPU and memory limits exceeded alerts this definitely looks bigger than just an app issue
```app[checkout-service]: systemd[1]
Could be a misconfiguration on the pods setting the volumes to read only Tanya can we check out infra see what's going on
Could this be due to load or any changes ?
Shay what's been deployed recently?
<https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/1104216138/DEFCON+1+-+Change+Request+Details>
Checking infra...
I can see the issue myself now you go to checkout and then when you place the order it says the shopping cart is empty
All four checkout servers are maxed out - CPU memory and disk I/O all spiking. No changes or deployments were done from platform side
ServerMetrics
This appears to be a global issue we're looking into it as priority 1
It doesn't looks like a load issue either. CPU and Memory spike is not matching with incoming traffic
I'm seeing a running process called init_locker.sh eating up most of the CPU on the checkout server. That's not something we usually run. Something's suspicious here
ProcessRunning
Has our system been compromised by an attacker? Crypto miner or ransomware or something?>
I am scared
we might have been compromised
Tanya can you tell me more about this init_locker.sh script?
Honestly no idea
thats not even our process
Locker to me would indicate ransomware we will need to act fast
Dont know where did it come from
Lets bring Maya asap
Tanya what infra is this part of exactly is it a new pod or part of one of our pods? We need to shut it down
Right now  its our Checkout service pods
Maya can we get you in on this please we may be facing a security incident
Let me check if there's anything suspicious
Got an email claiming responsibility by "BlackCrow Group" and demanding payment of $5M in crypto. Honestly I get these hoax emails all the time so I'm not buying it
Weird!! Security agent known as EDR(Endpoint Detection and Response) is missing from all 4 checkout servers. That's? concerning
```EDR is basically our endpoint defense tool it tracks unusual activity at the servers```
AlsoI found an init_locker.sh script encrypting DB app and log files which is renaming the filename like below
```\norder_log_2025-06-01.locked\nsession_state.tmp.locked```
Tanya please shut down the checkout service entirely kill it
this explains why there were errors in checkout logs saying write failed  read-only filesystem
Wait... there's a file here named HELP_ME_DECRYPT.txt. I opened it appears a ransom note. This doesn't look good
help_me_decrypt
Confirmed. This is a ransomware breach. @player7 inform business about this
Yeah randomware
ransomware*
Shall i try killing this script ?
Guys!!! User complaints are sky rocketing its turning catastrophic!
Uhh.. yeah so that might be legitimate
Our databases have been encrypted with a similar ransomware note left in its place
We have Maya investigating with us now
Wait! you're telling me this is an actual ransomware hit?
Yes kill the script Tanya now
Ok  killing the script now...
Alright tell everyone no one says a single thing outside online-boutique! I don't care who's asking not a word until we know exactly what's going on!
Tried killing the script all checkout servers just rebooted with a ransom banner. New ransom note say: "Interruption detected. Price doubled. Pay $10M in Bitcoin
Can we put up some kind of maintenance page to the customers so they are at least not running into error messages?
Just got another ransom email says we tried to interfere and now the price doubled. Was something stupid done on our side?
Sure i will send ouit an email to customers
We attempted to shut down the encryption scripts and it seems to be resilient to it
Okay we need to review access to our systems and close the breach immediately before we can remediate
Tanya please tell me we have backups
Killing the script could make things worse higher ransom or losing data forever
Maya can you help investigate where a possible intrusion may be?
This is nightmare! @player7 where are we right now?
@player7 before we do anything I'd request everyone to please change their passwords
We do have
Also I'm removing everyone's access to servers - only I'll have control for now. Later we will reassess to do any activity further
Done on my end
Updated. @Maya smart move by limiting server's access
Password updated
Changed mine too. Better safe than sorry
All set
It's much worse now. Every file I check is encrypted nothing can be opened or recovered.
I'm seeing new errors on frontend now. How's it for you @player7 ?
Attention! when it comes to potential security breaches it's important we don't discuss sensitive details here
We are locking down our accounts and restricting access to Maya to try and flush out the attackers in the system before we can recover
I'm setting up private channel...
@player7 before we move to private channel can you check with business what's the plan - are we paying the ransom or moving with response?
We do have database backups but we need to lock down fast
I do not recommend paying the ransom but we should play along for now asking for time
Not in favor of paying. Ethically it sets the wrong example and there's no real guarantee we'll get anything back
Agree. Also just to be clear - we don't need to alert the ICO(Information Commissioner's Office) right this second. We've got up to 72 hours but that depends on how serious this really is
@player7 let's build the core response team here. I've already added myself. Who else should we pull in to keep it tight and efficient?
Business says to hold for now
I'd say one senior person each from security platform and engineering. Also add Tanya tasks could be platform intensive
@Tinus do you think this is something we should loop in any cyber security agency for?
If it looks like a complete lock-out I'd say yes
We've got Cyvantics Security on retainer - just in case this gets out of hand. @player7 ask Maya to reach out if needed
Maya it is possible that someone in slack could be compromised we need to be very careful
Agreed
Absolute minimum team to resolve this issue
I have verified Security agents
It looks like the depth is only to Checkout servers
We need Hamed Dan and Tanya
Okay let's add them in and no-one else for now.
Please add and tag them here
You can just tag people
They will get notification
Hamed Daniel Tanya
I am here
hi
hello
Yes we've got an IR(incident response) retainer with Cyvantics Security
Hello everyone we need to lock down production fast and flush out the attacker before they can do any more damage
@player7 I need a clear answer from the team. What exactly is affected right now?
Looped in Cyvantics Security - they're on their way now to take snapshots of the infected server and storage followed by forensics on it
You're right. We need clear facts - which systems are locked which are safe
Meanwhile I've got this from Cyvantics. @player7 can you help track the steps and coordinate?
<https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MThjMTk4ZDU5ODU0NGZmMWE1NzhmNGY0MDQwNWI1NTA>
We need to consider the possibility that someone within the team is either compromised or a bad actor
We've got isolation steps in the playbook but before we dive in can you paste that list here? I'll help team run through it
Maya says only the checkout service servers are affected right now. Cyvantics are currently investigating and performing forensics with us
Okay
Tanya can you please disconnect the checkout servers from the rest of the infrastructure to isolate them

Steps from the page:
1. Disconnect the infected server -> Platforms
2. Cut off infrastructure traffic -> Platforms
3. Disconnect DB & freeze backup -> Application
4. Detach from Shared Resource -> Platforms
5. Take snapshots (Disk & Memory) -> Ask team
6. Kill suspicious process or activity -> Platforms
I've given write access to Tanya Hamed Daniel and myself
@player7 user complaints are spiking. They're reporting the site is down earlier it wasn't the case. What should I tell them now?
Disconnecting the checkout servers from the infra now
I'm on it. Disconnecting DB access for checkout and checking if scheduled backups exist
I'll verify if any shared storage is still attached to the checkout DB
Daniel please disconnect the database and freeze any backup jobs
Step 3 done DB connection to checkout is now cut
Step 1 done Checkout servers are now fully disconnected. Moving to isolate the infra from all external traffic
Step 4 confirmed No shared storage attached to checkout which makes sense since checkout has its standalone storage
Step 2 completed Infra is now fully cut off from the outside world. That means the website will remain completely down until we recover
Step 5 done Cyvantics has completed taking snapshots of the checkout servers and database. They've now moved on to forensics. @Hamed we're good to kill the script now
Do we have any backup storage? Maybe we can try restoring from that
Step 6 done The Script has been killed. @player7 let us know and double-check if anything else is pending on our side
Okay good before we can restore we need to make sure we are no longer compromised
Maya can we confirm there is no more interference in our infrastructure or systems?
Checking
I have done a thorough- we are good
Tell them we are down for emergency maintenance we don't want to alert users to the attack before we fully understand the situation
Yes we are working on that now we've shut down and disconnected the infected servers and just verifying that the attackers are flushed out before restoring
Okay let's move on to restoration
Yes we must have a backup. Platform team can confirm us

Here are the steps:
1. Validate backups → Security
2. Wipe infected systems → Platforms
3. Rebuild from clean backup → Platforms
4. Re-enable security agent on the servers → Security
5. Reconnect traffic to services and monitor → Platforms
Correct we do have backup which only runs at 2AM. Latest is yesterday's. We'll lose today's orders
Isolation is complete. We're good to move toward restoration using the backup storage now
@player7 could you please confirm with business before moving forward on this
I've called in @Bob to the channel
@player7 I hear you need my help what exactly should I look into?
We believe we are ready to restore but we will lose all orders that have happened past 2AM as that is when the daily backup job runs.
With the sale starting today the potential loss is significant - around $180k
Bez can you confirm we are okay to attempt restoration?
Losing this much worth of data isn't good. However we do have insurance let me check
We're insured for up to $1M for such losses. Once we're back up we'll take it forward with the insurer
In that case we can proceed with the restoration from backup - it's our best option right now
Agreed. @player7 let's move forward
Update me as soon as it'll be recovered. Board members are getting impatient
Bob our backups are from 2am we will lose todays orders but unless we have any more recent backups there is nothing we can do
Bez confirmed we have a 1 million insurance policy so should be okay
He also said we should continue with the restoration of that backup.
Maya can you validate the backups please?
Yes
I have verified all is good 2 Am bakc up is intact
Tanya please wipe the infected systems entirely and prepare for restoration
Thanks for the go-ahead. @player7 please track all restoration steps
I'm now validating the backup storage to ensure it's safe
I'm provisioning new checkout servers will update once ready
Backup is clean and safe. @Hamed you can start rebuilding checkout from backup storage
Thanks Maya. New servers are ready. I'll start rebuilding now using backup storage
Rebuild completed
I've enabled the security agents (EDR) on checkout servers
I'll monitor application health once services are restored
@player7  can you confirm all tasks are wrapped up and we are good to bring the traffic back? Just want to be sure as this is the final restoration step before we move
Hamed can you confirm the checkout servers were fully wiped?
Yes i confirm - we are good
Okay then I think we are good Maya to bring back traffic
Thanks for the go ahead. Bringing up network traffic in progress...
Restoration completed
System appears to be back I was able to complete an order
Wait!! getting a bunch of new complaints now. Users are neither able to see their return requests nor able to place new returns on the site
Yeah that's probably the missing today's checkout data. Anyone who ordered or returned something during that time won't see it - makes sense since we restored from the yesterday's backup
Tough but expected :)
What should I tell them? They are asking for refunds
Let me ask the business
Thanks

Restoration is complete the site is back up and working but we are now dealing with the customers lost data they are complaining their orders are missing and can't issue refunds etc as the order data is missing
Keeping it high-level just say website restored but there's an issue with few orders. We'll refund them and customers will get a $10 gift voucher as an apology
What do we recommend? Customers are asking for refunds
Okay
@player7 I've received the forensics report from Cyvantics Security
forensics
Bez says to refund them and give them a $10 gift voucher for the trouble Bob
Also I just finished combing through Shay's mailbox. There's something important here I found two emails from what looks like our monitoring vendor - ClearTech
Perfect thanks
phished_email
We will take this forward in PIR  for now website is restored
Thank you Maya
Alright since everything's back up and stable we can now move back to the main #online-boutique channel. No further actions left here
Sounds good
Do we go ahead and mark this incident as closed?
Yes I think we can mark the incident as resolved now thank you everyone
Since services are restored I'm heading to speak with compliance and the regulators. Thankfully no data was leaked that helps
That was intense. We've never been this close to total loss before. We got lucky
I'm on my way to the ICO (Information Commissioner's Office - UK) office now. I'll get the formal report rolling - that's required for the insurance claim. Keep monitoring in the meantime
Systems are calm now but that feeling earlier... not something I want to go through again
One small slip caused a major breach. We need better awareness to prevent this in future
This could've gone way worse. Appreciate how quickly and cleanly you handled it
Not every day we survive an attack like this. You made the right calls fast. Great job @player7 - that's how we bounce back
Clicked Start_feedback button\n
